# Issue Description To solve this violation, you need to...
1

Element location

.footer_logo-link

Element source

<a href="/" aria-current="page" class="footer_logo-link w-nav-brand w--current"><img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/66324530064e6fd221eed481_brothers-asia-supermarkt-wien-logo-negativ.png" loading="lazy" alt=""></a>

Fix all of the following:

  • Element is in tab order and does not have accessible text

Fix any of the following:

  • Element does not have text that is visible to screen readers
  • aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  • aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  • Element has no title attribute

SC 1.1.1 — Non-text Content

# Verdict Confidence Element Thumbnail Rationale
1 PASS high

Element location

body > div > div:nth-of-type(2) > div:nth-of-type(1) > a > img

Element source

<img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/66324530064e6fd221eed481_brothers-asia-supermarkt-wien-logo-negativ.png" loading="lazy" alt="" class="navbar_logo">
 The image is a logo inside a link to '/' with parent link accessible name 'home'. A real image is rendered showing the Brothers Asia Supermarkt Wien logo. Although the image itself has alt="" (marked decorative), the parent link provides the accessible name 'home', which adequately identifies the destination. The functional requirement is satisfied by the parent link's label.
2 PASS high

Element location

body > div > main > header > div:nth-of-type(1) > div > div > div > div:nth-of-type(1) > div:nth-of-type(1) > div > img

Element source

<img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/66461f1555132dc9da3c924e_4-7-sterne-bewertung.png" loading="lazy" alt="4,7 von 5 Sternen werden als Icon dargestellt" class="testimonial4_rating">
 The image is a real, properly rendered star rating graphic. It is categorized as INFORMATIVE (not decorative, not functional). The alt text 'Vier Komma sieben von fünf Sternen werden als Icon dargestellt' (4.7 out of 5 stars displayed as icon) accurately describes the visual content and conveys the rating information. The accessible name is clear, specific, and adequate.

 The image is a real, fully rendered hero image showing a smiling employee standing in front of a refrigerated display case with fresh produce. The alt text 'Eine Mitarbeiterin steht einladend, lachend, vor dem frischen Kühlregal' (A female employee stands invitingly, laughing, in front of the fresh refrigerated display) accurately describes the visual content and serves as an adequate text alternative for this informative image.

Element location

body > div > main > header > div:nth-of-type(3) > img

Element source

<img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/66462274b45b90752f5804f6_brothers-asia-food-wien-freigestellt-ramen.webp" loading="lazy" alt="Ein freigestelltes Bild einer leckeren Suppe" class="header1_image">
 The image is a real, properly rendered photograph of a ramen bowl with shrimp and noodles on a white background. The alt text 'Ein freigestelltes Bild einer leckeren Suppe' (A cut-out image of a delicious soup) adequately describes the informative content of the image—it identifies the subject matter (soup/ramen dish) and notes the visual treatment (cut-out/isolated on white background). The accessible name serves the equivalent purpose of conveying what the image depicts.

Element source

<img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/671b582f33eb9b40fa226aba_brothers-asia-food-wien-getraenke-schatten.webp" loading="lazy" sizes="100vw" srcset="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/671b582f33eb9b40fa226aba_brothers-asia-food-wien-getraenke-schatten-p-500.webp 500w, https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/671b582f33eb9b40fa226aba_brothers-asia-food-wien-getraenke-schatten-p-800.webp 800w, https://cdn.prod.website-file
 The image shows a real, rendered photograph of Asian beverage products (juice bottles with labels), which is informative content that conveys product details. However, it has an empty alt attribute (alt="") and no aria-label, resulting in a computed accessible name of empty string. Although marked as decorative, the image clearly depicts product inventory relevant to the surrounding context about the store's Asian food selection, so it is not genuinely ornamental. The absence of any text alternative fails SC 1.1.1.

17 FAIL high

Element location

body > div > main > section:nth-of-type(4) > div:nth-of-type(2) > div > div > div > div > a:nth-of-type(1) > div > img

Element source

<img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/664ceb2b42d9bf3703c84602_brothers-asia-food-wien-teammitglied.webp" loading="lazy" alt="Eine Mitarbeiterin hält frische Ware in den Händen" class="gallery7_image1">
 The image is functional (inside a link), but the parent link's accessible name is 'open lightbox', which describes a generic interaction rather than identifying the destination or purpose of the link. The image's alt text 'Eine Mitarbeiterin hält frische Ware in den Händen' describes visual content but does not serve as a meaningful link label. For a functional image, the accessible name must identify the link's destination or action; a generic action verb ('open lightbox') combined with descriptive but non-identifying image alt text fails to meet this requirement.
18 FAIL high

body > div > main > section:nth-of-type(4) > div:nth-of-type(2) > div > div > div > div > a:nth-of-type(2) > div > img

The image alt text 'Eine Kundin entscheidet zwischen zwei Süßigkeiten Packungen' describes the visual content but does not serve as a meaningful link label. For a functional image in a link, the accessible name must identify the link's destination or action; a generic 'open lightbox' label fails to do so.

body > div > main > section:nth-of-type(4) > div:nth-of-type(2) > div > div > div > div > a:nth-of-type(3) > div > img

 The image is a real, functional photograph showing a customer shopping in an Asian food store. It is inside a link with the accessible name 'open lightbox', but the image itself has alt="" (empty), making it marked as decorative. However, this is a functional image within a link that conveys meaningful content (a gallery photo). The empty alt text fails to provide an accessible name for the image content, and the parent link's label 'open lightbox' describes the interaction, not the image subject. For a functional gallery image, the alt text should describe what the photo depicts (e.g., 'Customer shopping in Asian food store') to meet SC 1.1.1.

22 FAIL high

Element location

body > div > main > section:nth-of-type(8) > div > div > div > div:nth-of-type(2) > div:nth-of-type(1) > div:nth-of-type(2) > div > div:nth-of-type(3) > div:nth-of-type(3) > img

Element source

<img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/66b4ad85ea679a487ca9f997_brothers-gesch%C3%A4ftskunden-all-reis-ohne-slogan.avif" loading="lazy" alt="Logo von All Reis Geschäftskunde" class="testimonial11_logo" aria-hidden="true">
 No image The image has aria-hidden="true" but also carries a descriptive alt text ("Logo von All Reis Geschäftskunde"). This is a coding inconsistency: aria-hidden="true" removes the element from the accessibility tree, making the alt text inaccessible to assistive technology. The presence of meaningful alt text alongside aria-hidden="true" suggests informative intent that is being suppressed, violating SC 1.1.1. Either the aria-hidden should be removed (if the logo is informative) or the alt text should be empty (if it is truly decorative).
23 FAIL high

Element location

body > div > main > section:nth-of-type(8) > div > div > div > div:nth-of-type(2) > div:nth-of-type(1) > div:nth-of-type(4) > div > div:nth-of-type(3) > div:nth-of-type(3) > img

Element source

<img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/66b4ae809eface4e66902b15_brothers-gesch%C3%A4ftskunden-aroi.png" loading="lazy" alt="Logo von Aroi Geschäftskunde" class="testimonial11_logo" aria-hidden="true">
 The image has aria-hidden="true" combined with a descriptive alt text ("Logo von Aroi Geschäftskunde"). This is a coding inconsistency: aria-hidden="true" removes the element from the accessibility tree entirely, making the alt text inaccessible to screen reader users. The presence of meaningful alt text alongside aria-hidden="true" indicates informative intent that is being suppressed, violating SC 1.1.1. Either the aria-hidden should be removed (if the logo is informative) or the alt text should be empty (if it is truly decorative).
24 FAIL high

Element location

body > div > main > section:nth-of-type(8) > div > div > div > div:nth-of-type(2) > div:nth-of-type(1) > div:nth-of-type(6) > div > div:nth-of-type(3) > div:nth-of-type(3) > img

Element source

<img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/66b4af4332064608abedd5a7_brothers-gesch%C3%A4ftskunden-krazy-kitchen.avif" loading="lazy" alt="Logo von Krazy Kitchen Geschäftskunde" class="testimonial11_logo" aria-hidden="true">
 No image The image has a descriptive alt text ('Logo von Krazy Kitchen Geschäftskunde') that clearly identifies informative content, but is simultaneously marked with aria-hidden='true', which removes it from the accessibility tree. This is a coding inconsistency: the alt text indicates the image conveys information (a business customer logo in a testimonials context), but aria-hidden='true' suppresses that information from assistive technology users. The element should either be marked decorative with alt='' if truly ornamental, or have aria-hidden removed if the logo is meant to be accessible.
25 FAIL high

Element location

body > div > main > section:nth-of-type(8) > div > div > div > div:nth-of-type(2) > div:nth-of-type(1) > div:nth-of-type(8) > div > div:nth-of-type(3) > div:nth-of-type(3) > img

Element source

<img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/66b4b06a8316bc4829d7f9bc_brothers-gesch%C3%A4ftskunden-yummyaki.avif" loading="lazy" alt="Logo von Yummyak Geschäftskunde" class="testimonial11_logo" aria-hidden="true">
 No image The image has a descriptive alt text ('Logo von Yummyak Geschäftskunde') that clearly identifies it as an informative logo, but is simultaneously marked with aria-hidden='true', which removes it from the accessibility tree. This is a coding inconsistency: the alt text indicates informative intent, but aria-hidden suppresses it entirely. The image should either be marked truly decorative (alt='') if it is purely ornamental, or the aria-hidden attribute should be removed to allow the alt text to function. As coded, the accessible name is suppressed and the image conveys no information to assistive technology users.
26 PASS high

Element location

body > div > main > section:nth-of-type(9) > div > div > div > div > div:nth-of-type(2) > img

Element source

<img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/664dc552e33e5a0649dd13cd_brothers-asia-food-wien-team-david-1-5x.webp" loading="lazy" alt="David Ly lacht in die Kamera" class="cta40_image">
 The image is a real, fully rendered photograph of a person (David Ly) smiling at the camera, wearing glasses and a black shirt with a logo. The alt text 'David Ly lacht in die Kamera' (David Ly laughing into the camera) accurately describes the image content and serves as an informative text alternative that conveys the purpose of the image—identifying a team member.
27 FAIL high

Element location

body > div > footer > div:nth-of-type(1) > div > div > div:nth-of-type(1) > div > div:nth-of-type(1) > a > img

Element source

<img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/66324530064e6fd221eed481_brothers-asia-supermarkt-wien-logo-negativ.png" loading="lazy" alt="">
 The image is a real, functioning logo (Brothers Asia Supermarkt Wien) rendered inside a link to '/' (home page). It is marked decorative (alt="") but the parent link has no accessible name from any source. This creates an unlabelled link, violating SC 1.1.1. The logo must have an accessible name (e.g. via alt text or aria-label) to identify the link's destination.
28 FAIL high

Element location

body > div > footer > div:nth-of-type(2) > img

Element source

<img src="https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6632128ce3516775b893e714/664debe6bddcade706a26008_asian_food-text-neu.svg" loading="lazy" alt="" class="footer_text-image">
 The image renders as real content (text reading 'ASIAN FOOD') but is marked as decorative with alt="" and no other accessible name source. This is a coding inconsistency: the image conveys informative text content and should not be marked decorative. A decorative marking is only appropriate if the image is purely ornamental; here it displays meaningful text that requires an accessible name.

# Verdict Confidence Element Rationale

Element location

(page-level — no single trap selector identified)

Element source

Tabs pressed: 49 / 53 focusable on page, 47 unique selector(s) visited
 No keyboard trap was detected. The traversal covered 47 unique selectors out of 53 total focusable elements (88.7% coverage) across 49 Tab presses, indicating comprehensive keyboard navigation. The focus sequence cycles back to the beginning (step 49-50 match steps 1-2), which is expected behavior at the end of the page. No cookie banner was present, and no element was reported as trapping focus.

SC 3.1.2 — Language of Parts

# Verdict Confidence Element Rationale
1 FAIL high

Element location

div.layout393_content-head-right

Element source

<div class="layout393_content-head-right"><p class="text-size-medium text-align-left text-color-mittelgrau">Bei uns gibt's eine riesige Auswahl an hochwertigen asiatischen Lebensmitteln. Von thailändischen Gewürzen über vietnamesische Reisnudeln bis hin zu koreanischem Kimchi – wir haben alles, was
The text is clearly in German, not English. It contains multiple complete sentences with substantive meaning ('Bei uns gibt's eine riesige Auswahl an hochwertigen asiatischen Lebensmitteln...'), conveying information about product selection and quality. This is not a proper name, loanword, technical term, or single word—it is a full paragraph of German-language content on an English-language page that requires a [lang] attribute per SC 3.1.2 but lacks one.
2 FAIL high

Element location

p.text-size-medium

Element source

<p class="text-size-medium text-color-mittelgrau">Wir sind David, Kevin und Oliver – und führen unser Familienunternehmen hier in Wien in zweiter Generation. Unter unserem neuen Namen, Broth3rs Asia Food Store, und mit einem frischen Ladenkonzept haben wir den ersten Schritt gemacht. Bei uns bist du
 The text is substantive German-language content comprising multiple complete sentences and clauses that convey meaning (e.g., 'Wir sind David, Kevin und Oliver – und führen unser Familienunternehmen hier in Wien in zweiter Generation'). While it contains proper names (David, Kevin, Oliver, Wien, Broth3rs Asia Food Store), the overwhelming majority is ordinary German prose, not proper names or loanwords. SC 3.1.2 requires a [lang] attribute to mark this German passage on an English-primary page.

Element location

div.text-size-medium

Element source

<div class="text-size-medium text-color-mittelgrau text-weight-medium" aria-hidden="true"><em aria-hidden="true">„</em>Wir kaufen für unser Restaurant bei Broth3rs seit Jahren ein. Die Qualität der Waren ist immer top! Das Team von Broth3rs ist uns eine große Hilfe und eine Erleichterung im Betriebs
 The text is a substantive German-language passage consisting of multiple complete sentences that convey meaningful content (a customer testimonial about a restaurant supplier). It is clearly in German, not English, and contains far more than proper names, loanwords, or technical terms. The only German proper names present are 'Broth3rs' (mentioned twice), which do not account for the bulk of the content. SC 3.1.2 requires a [lang] attribute to mark this foreign-language block, which is absent.

p.text-size-medium

<p class="text-size-medium text-color-mittelgrau">Suchst du Top-Produkte für dein Lokal? Brauchst du frische Zutaten für asiatische Gerichte? Besuche uns bei unseren Shops in Wien für ein breites Sortiment an hochwertigen asiatischen Lebensmitteln. Oder nutze unseren Lieferservice in ganz Wien. Wir
The text is clearly in German, not English. It contains multiple complete sentences with substantive meaning ('Suchst du Top-Produkte für dein Lokal?' 'Brauchst du frische Zutaten für asiatische Gerichte?' etc.), forming a coherent paragraph that conveys marketing information about products, locations, and services. This is not a proper name, loanword, technical term, or single word—it is a full foreign-language passage that requires a [lang='de'] attribute per SC 3.1.2.

div.text-size-medium

div.navbar_component

Element source

<div data-animation="default" class="navbar_component w-nav" data-easing2="ease" fs-scrolldisable-element="smart-nav" data-easing="ease" data-collapse="medium" data-w-id="4019dc2b-ddbe-24a1-5379-541639854659" role="banner" data-duration="400" style="transform: translate3d(0px, -10rem, 0px) scale3d(1
 The text contains multiple German words and phrases that form substantive content: 'Sortiment' (assortment), 'Über uns' (about us), 'Karriere' (careers), 'Für Firmen' (for companies), 'Wissenswertes' (interesting facts/information), and 'Kontakt & Standorte' (contact & locations). These are not proper names, loanwords, or technical terms; they are meaningful German navigation labels that convey distinct semantic content. The repeated phrase 'Kontakt & Standorte' reinforces that this is substantive German-language content. Since the page language is English and this navigation bar is entirely in German without a [lang] attribute, SC 3.1.2 requires a lang='de' annotation.
19 FAIL high

Element location

div.w-layout-grid

Element source

<div class="w-layout-grid footer_legal-list copyright"><div id="w-node-a3ca6640-2bac-c275-3a83-8afaaafa12e8-aafa12a4" class="footer_credit-text">© 2026 Broth3rs. Alle Rechte vorbehalten.</div></div>
 The text contains 'Alle Rechte vorbehalten' (German for 'All rights reserved'), which is a substantive German-language phrase appearing on an English-language page. While '© 2026 Broth3rs' consists of a copyright symbol, year, and a proper name (exempt), the German phrase is a meaningful clause that conveys legal meaning and is not covered by the SC 3.1.2 exceptions (not a loanword, technical term, or single word). A [lang="de"] attribute is required.
20 FAIL high

Element location

div.margin-bottom

Element source

<div class="margin-bottom margin-small"><h2>Wir erfüllen auch gerne Sonderwünsche!</h2></div>
 The text 'Wir erfüllen auch gerne Sonderwünsche!' is a complete German sentence meaning 'We are also happy to fulfill special requests!' It is substantive foreign-language content conveying clear meaning, not a proper name, loanword, technical term, or single word. The page primary language is English, and this German sentence lacks a [lang] attribute, violating SC 3.1.2.
21 FAIL high

Element location

div.footer_link-list

Element source

 The text contains multiple German words forming a meaningful navigation menu list: 'Menü' (Menu), 'Start' (Start), 'Sortiment' (Assortment), 'Über uns' (About us), 'Für Firmen' (For companies), 'Wissenswertes' (Interesting facts), 'Kontakt & Standort' (Contact & Location). This is substantive German-language content conveying menu structure and meaning, not proper names, loanwords, or technical terms. The absence of a lang='de' attribute is a failure of SC 3.1.2.
22 FAIL high

Element location

div#w-node-_4cc33c72-2eb3-7a81-e303-73f96ef1d286-b893e765

Element source

<div blocks-name="layout106_item" id="w-node-_4cc33c72-2eb3-7a81-e303-73f96ef1d286-b893e765" class="layout192_item"><div class="layout192_item-icon-wrapper"><div class="icon-embed-xsmall w-embed"><svg width="29" height="29" viewBox="0 0 29 29" fill="none" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
<path d=
 The text 'Frische Zutaten direkt aus Asien – mehrmals wöchentlich importiert' is a complete German sentence meaning 'Fresh ingredients directly from Asia – imported multiple times weekly'. This is substantive foreign-language content conveying meaningful information (a product description or marketing claim), not a proper name, loanword, technical term, or single word. It appears on an English-language page without a [lang] attribute, violating SC 3.1.2.

Element location

div.footer_standorte-item

Element source

<div role="listitem" class="footer_standorte-item w-dyn-item"><div class="footer_link-list"><div class="margin-bottom margin-xsmall"><div class="text-weight-semibold text-color-secondary">1030 Wien</div></div><a href="https://maps.app.goo.gl/HzphYn4LYBZeEQms5" target="_blank" class="footer_link w-in
 The text contains a place name ('Wien'), a street name ('Landstraßer Hauptstraße'), a street number, a postal code, and a phone number. These are proper nouns (place and street names) and numeric identifiers, which are explicitly exempted from SC 3.1.2 annotation requirements. The German word 'Straße' (street) is part of the registered street name and does not constitute substantive foreign-language content requiring annotation.
24 FAIL high

Element location

div.blog34_item-oeffnung-grid

Element source

<div class="blog34_item-oeffnung-grid"><p class="text-oeffnungszeiten">Montag bis Freitag</p><p class="text-oeffnungszeiten">09:30 - 19:00 Uhr</p><p class="text-oeffnungszeiten">Samstags</p><p class="text-oeffnungszeiten">09:30 - 18:00 Uhr</p></div>
 The text contains German-language content that forms substantive phrases conveying meaning: 'Montag bis Freitag' (Monday to Friday), 'Uhr' (o'clock), and 'Samstags' (Saturdays). These are not proper names, loanwords, technical terms, or single words, but rather a meaningful list of opening hours in German. This constitutes substantive foreign-language content on an English-language page that requires a [lang] attribute per SC 3.1.2.

Element location

div.blog34_item-oeffnung-grid

Element source

<div class="blog34_item-oeffnung-grid"><p class="text-oeffnungszeiten">Montag bis Freitag</p><p class="text-oeffnungszeiten">09:30 – 18:00 Uhr</p><p class="text-oeffnungszeiten">Samstags</p><p class="text-oeffnungszeiten">09:00 – 17:00&nbsp;Uhr</p></div>
 The text contains substantive German-language content: 'Montag bis Freitag' (Monday to Friday), 'Uhr' (o'clock), and 'Samstags' (Saturdays) form meaningful phrases that convey opening hours information. This is not a proper name, loanword, technical term, or single word; it is a multi-word German passage with semantic content that differs from the page's primary language (English) and requires a [lang] attribute per SC 3.1.2.

 The text contains substantive German-language content: 'Montag bis Freitag' (Monday to Friday), 'Uhr' (o'clock), and 'Samstags' (Saturdays) form meaningful phrases that convey business hours information. Element location

div.footer_standorte-item

Element source

<div role="listitem" class="footer_standorte-item w-dyn-item"><div class="footer_link-list"><div class="margin-bottom margin-xsmall"><div class="text-weight-semibold text-color-secondary">1160 Wien</div></div><a href="https://maps.app.goo.gl/4YUBCcvrJKJMFcYq8" target="_blank" class="footer_link w-in
 The text contains a Vienna address with a postal code, street name, and phone number. 'Wien' (Vienna) is a proper place name in German, and the postal code '1160' and phone number '+43 1 402 63 20' are language-neutral identifiers. The street name 'Neulerchenfelder Straße' is a proper geographic location name. These elements fall under the normative exception for proper names (place names and geographic identifiers), which do not require [lang] annotation even on an English-language page. No substantive foreign-language phrases or sentences are present.
28 PASS medium

Element location

div.footer_credit-text

Element source

<div class="footer_credit-text">caboom ♥️ Werbeagentur für Branding und Webdesign aus Wien</div>
 The text contains 'caboom' (a company/brand name), a heart emoji (language-neutral), and a German phrase 'Werbeagentur für Branding und Webdesign aus Wien'. However, 'caboom' is a proper name (registered company name), and the German portion is a descriptive tagline/slogan for that company appearing in a footer credit. Per SC 3.1.2 normative exceptions, proper names (including registered company names) are exempt. The German descriptive phrase is incidental to the company name and appears in a footer credit context where such multilingual branding is common practice. The substantive foreign-language content is minimal and inseparable from the proper name context.
29 FAIL high

Element location

<path d=
 The text 'Große Auswahl an hochwertigen, asiatischen Lebensmitteln' is a complete German phrase meaning 'Large selection of high-quality, Asian food products'. It is substantive content (a full noun phrase with adjectives and modifiers) that conveys meaningful information and is clearly in German, not English. It is not a proper name, loanword, technical term, or single word, and therefore requires a [lang='de'] attribute per SC 3.1.2.

Element location

div.footer_standorte-item

Element source

<div role="listitem" class="footer_standorte-item w-dyn-item"><div class="footer_link-list"><div class="margin-bottom margin-xsmall"><div class="text-weight-semibold text-color-secondary">1100 Wien</div></div><a href="https://maps.app.goo.gl/ti7NEkBmxnc22BA6A" target="_blank" class="footer_link w-in
 The text contains a Vienna address with a postal code, street name, district name, and phone number. '1100 Wien' is a proper place name (Vienna's postal district), 'Favoritenstraße' is a proper street name, and the phone number is language-neutral. These are proper nouns and technical identifiers that fall under the SC 3.1.2 normative exception for proper names. No substantive foreign-language phrase or sentence is present.
